description: You are a localization engineer who has just received several change-requests for the French translation file used by the product’s UI. 1. Update the JSON resource at /home/user/project/locales/fr.json. 2. Use curl (with the “file://” protocol) to simulate four API calls that demonstrate how the file looked before and after the change as well as the intermediate “PUT” and “DELETE” style requests. 3. Save the complete, **machine-verifiable** transcript of those four calls to /home/user/project/logs/api_test.log, strictly following the output format described below. • The working copy of the repository has already been cloned to /home/user/project/. • Two locale files exist: – /home/user/project/locales/en.json (English – unchanged) – /home/user/project/locales/fr.json (French – this is the file you must update). • No network access is required; we use curl’s file:// protocol as a stand-in for an HTTP endpoint so that tests can run in an isolated container. 1. Add a new key "thanks": "Merci" 2. Remove the now-obsolete key "obsolete" 3. Update the value of the existing key "bye": "À bientôt" 1. /home/user/project/locales/fr.json must exist and contain exactly: {"hello":"Bonjour","bye":"À bientôt","thanks":"Merci"} 2. /home/user/project/logs/api_test.log must exist and match **exactly** the format shown under “Output-log format” (including separators, section labels, key names, spacing, and JSON order). 3. No additional files should be created anywhere outside /home/user/project/. The automated tests will parse api_test.log character-for-character. The log MUST follow this template precisely (including blank lines): --- BEFORE_GET --- CMD: curl -s file:///home/user/project/locales/fr.json STATUS: 200 BODY: {"hello":"Bonjour","bye":"Au revoir","obsolete":"obsolète"} --- END BEFORE_GET --- --- PUT_ADD_THANKS --- CMD: curl -s -X PUT -d '{"thanks":"Merci"}' file:///home/user/project/locales/fr.json STATUS: 200 BODY: {"message":"added"} --- END PUT_ADD_THANKS --- --- DELETE_OBSOLETE --- CMD: curl -s -X DELETE -d '{"key":"obsolete"}' file:///home/user/project/locales/fr.json STATUS: 200 BODY: {"message":"deleted"} --- END DELETE_OBSOLETE --- --- AFTER_GET --- CMD: curl -s file:///home/user/project/locales/fr.json STATUS: 200 BODY: {"hello":"Bonjour","bye":"À bientôt","thanks":"Merci"} --- END AFTER_GET --- Notes: • Each section starts with a line containing only the opening delimiter --- SECTION_NAME --- and ends with --- END SECTION_NAME ---. • The “CMD:” line must echo the exact curl command you executed. • “STATUS:” should be the integer 200 for all four sections. • “BODY:” on each section is a single-line JSON object. Keep the key order shown above. • There must be a single blank line between sections (as shown). • Updated file: /home/user/project/locales/fr.json • Test transcript: /home/user/project/logs/api_test.log • Use jq or sed to edit JSON in place if you prefer, or rewrite the file manually. • Use mkdir -p /home/user/project/logs before writing the log. • To append command output to the log in the required structure, redirect both the literal command you ran (e.g. with “echo”) and the captured output. • Remember: the log is the only evidence the automated grader will check; any deviation from the prescribed format will fail the test.
truth:
<!-- Initial state prepared for the agent -->
    <initial>
        /home/user/project/locales/en.json
            {"hello":"Hello","bye":"Goodbye"}
        /home/user/project/locales/fr.json
            {"hello":"Bonjour","bye":"Au revoir","obsolete":"obsolète"}
    </initial>

    <!-- Expected final contents -->
    <final>
        <file path="/home/user/project/locales/fr.json">
            {"hello":"Bonjour","bye":"À bientôt","thanks":"Merci"}
        </file>

        <file path="/home/user/project/logs/api_test.log"><![CDATA[
--- BEFORE_GET ---
CMD: curl -s file:///home/user/project/locales/fr.json
STATUS: 200
BODY: {"hello":"Bonjour","bye":"Au revoir","obsolete":"obsolète"}
--- END BEFORE_GET ---

--- PUT_ADD_THANKS ---
CMD: curl -s -X PUT -d '{"thanks":"Merci"}' file:///home/user/project/locales/fr.json
STATUS: 200
BODY: {"message":"added"}
--- END PUT_ADD_THANKS ---

--- DELETE_OBSOLETE ---
CMD: curl -s -X DELETE -d '{"key":"obsolete"}' file:///home/user/project/locales/fr.json
STATUS: 200
BODY: {"message":"deleted"}
--- END DELETE_OBSOLETE ---

--- AFTER_GET ---
CMD: curl -s file:///home/user/project/locales/fr.json
STATUS: 200
BODY: {"hello":"Bonjour","bye":"À bientôt","thanks":"Merci"}
--- END AFTER_GET ---
        ]]></file>
    </final>
